diagnosis: This is a Layer 2-style setup/configuration failure: the configured local model directory (`.../models/PubMedBERT-base`) exists but does not contain any Hugging Face checkpoint weight files (`model.safetensors`, `pytorch_model.bin`, or shard index). `train.py` then retries `from_pretrained` with the same invalid local path, so it fails again. Root cause is an invalid/incomplete model artifact path combined with non-robust model-loading fallback logic.

patches.old:
def build_model_and_tokenizer(args, device: torch.device):
    try:
        tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(args.model_path, local_files_only=True)
        model = AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained(
            args.model_path,
            num_labels=3,
            id2label=ID2LABEL,
            label2id=LABEL2ID,
            ignore_mismatched_sizes=True,
            local_files_only=True,
        )
    except Exception:
        tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(args.model_path)
        model = AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained(
            args.model_path,
            num_labels=3,
            id2label=ID2LABEL,
            label2id=LABEL2ID,
            ignore_mismatched_sizes=True,
        )
    model.to(device)
    return model, tokenizer
patches.description: Makes model loading robust to incomplete local checkpoints by validating local weight files first, trying a local BioBERT fallback, then trying known HF model IDs instead of retrying the same broken path.

patches.old:
if not os.path.isdir(args.model_path):
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Model path not found: {args.model_path}")
patches.description: Prevents premature hard failure when the provided model argument is not a local directory, allowing fallback loading logic to run.
